Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class MainWindowViewModel

Namespace
Pandowdy.UI.ViewModels
Assembly
Pandowdy.UI.dll

Main view model for the application window, coordinating child view models and UI state.

public sealed class MainWindowViewModel : ReactiveObject, IReactiveNotifyPropertyChanged<IReactiveObject>, IHandleObservableErrors, IReactiveObject, INotifyPropertyChanged, INotifyPropertyChanging, IEnableLogger
Inheritance
object
ReactiveObject
MainWindowViewModel

IReactiveNotifyPropertyChanged<IReactiveObject>
IHandleObservableErrors
IReactiveObject

INotifyPropertyChanging

object.GetType()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Architecture: This view model serves as the root composition point for all child view models and coordinates application-level state and commands. It follows the MVVM pattern with ReactiveUI for property change notifications and command execution.

Child View Models:

  • EmulatorState: Displays CPU state (PC, cycles, BASIC line)
  • SystemStatus: Displays soft switches and system status

Responsibility Separation: This view model manages UI-level state (menu toggles, display options) but delegates emulator control to IEmulatorState. The view (MainWindow.axaml.cs) bridges between these commands and actual emulator services.

Command Pattern: Uses ReactiveCommand for all user actions, enabling easy binding to menu items and keyboard shortcuts while maintaining testability.

Constructors

MainWindowViewModel(EmulatorStateViewModel, IEmulatorState, SystemStatusViewModel)

Initializes a new instance of the MainWindowViewModel class.

public MainWindowViewModel(EmulatorStateViewModel emulatorState, IEmulatorState emuState, SystemStatusViewModel systemStatus)

Parameters

emulatorState EmulatorStateViewModel

View model for displaying CPU state.

emuState IEmulatorState

Emulator state provider for control commands.

systemStatus SystemStatusViewModel

View model for displaying system status.

Remarks

Dependency Injection: All dependencies are injected via constructor, enabling testability and loose coupling. Child view models are created and managed by the DI container.

Command Initialization: All ReactiveCommands are initialized in the constructor. Commands that modify properties use inline lambdas for simplicity.

Future View Models: ErrorLog and Disassembly view models are commented out pending implementation of those features.

Placeholder Commands: StartEmu, StopEmu, ResetEmu, and StepOnce are currently empty commands. The view (MainWindow.axaml.cs) handles these actions directly. Future refactoring may move this logic into the view model for better separation of concerns and testability.

Properties

CapsLockEnabled

Gets or sets whether Caps Lock emulation is enabled.

public bool CapsLockEnabled { get; set; }

Property Value

bool

True to enable Caps Lock behavior, false to disable.

Remarks

When enabled, keyboard input is converted to uppercase (matching Apple IIe behavior). When disabled, keyboard input preserves case.

The Apple IIe keyboard was uppercase-only (no lowercase letters in ROM character set). This option allows modern users to type naturally while maintaining authentic behavior.

ContinueCommand

Gets the command to continue emulator execution after pause.

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ContinueCommand { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that requests the emulator to resume running.

Remarks

When executed, calls IEmulatorState.RequestContinue() which signals the emulator to resume continuous execution (restart RunAsync loop).

DecreaseContrast

Gets or sets whether to decrease display contrast for a softer appearance.

public bool DecreaseContrast { get; set; }

Property Value

bool

True to decrease contrast, false for normal contrast.

Remarks

Reduces the intensity difference between bright and dark pixels, simulating an aged CRT monitor or reducing eye strain.

EmulatorState

Gets the view model for displaying emulator CPU state.

public EmulatorStateViewModel EmulatorState { get; }

Property Value

EmulatorStateViewModel

View model showing PC, cycles, and BASIC line number.

ForceMonochrome

Gets or sets whether to force monochrome (green or amber) display mode.

public bool ForceMonochrome { get; set; }

Property Value

bool

True to force monochrome, false for color display.

Remarks

When enabled, renders output in monochrome (simulating a green or amber phosphor monitor). When disabled, uses NTSC color artifact emulation.

Many early Apple IIe users had monochrome monitors which were sharper and easier to read for text. This option replicates that experience.

MonoMixed

Gets or sets whether to use monochrome rendering in mixed text/graphics mode.

public bool MonoMixed { get; set; }

Property Value

bool

True for monochrome mixed mode, false for color.

Remarks

Mixed mode displays graphics with 4 lines of text at the bottom. This option controls whether the graphics portion is rendered in monochrome or color.

Some programs look better in monochrome mixed mode, particularly those designed for monochrome monitors.

PauseCommand

Gets the command to pause emulator execution.

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> PauseCommand { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that requests the emulator to pause (RunAsync loop exits).

Remarks

When executed, calls IEmulatorState.RequestPause() which signals the emulator to stop continuous execution. The emulator remains initialized and can be resumed with ContinueCommand.

ResetEmu

Gets the command to reset the emulator (full system reset / power cycle).

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ResetEmu { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that performs a cold boot reset.

Remarks

Implementation Note: This command is currently a no-op placeholder. The actual reset logic is handled by the view which calls VA2M.Reset() directly.

Future enhancement may move reset control into the view model for better testability.

ShowScanLines

Gets or sets whether CRT scanline effect is shown on the display.

public bool ShowScanLines { get; set; }

Property Value

bool

True to show scanlines, false for smooth display.

Remarks

When enabled, adds alternating dark lines to simulate the appearance of a CRT monitor. When disabled, shows a clean, modern display.

This is a cosmetic option for nostalgia/authenticity. Does not affect emulation accuracy.

ShowSoftSwitchStatus

Gets or sets whether the soft switch status panel is visible.

public bool ShowSoftSwitchStatus { get; set; }

Property Value

bool

True to show the status panel, false to hide it.

Remarks

Controls the visibility of the right-side panel displaying Apple IIe soft switch states (memory mapping, video modes, ROM selection, annunciators, pushbuttons).

Users can toggle this to maximize screen space for the display or show detailed system status for debugging and learning.

StartEmu

Gets the command to start emulator execution.

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> StartEmu { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that initiates continuous emulation.

Remarks

Implementation Note: This command is currently a no-op placeholder. The actual start logic is handled by the view (MainWindow.axaml.cs) which calls VA2M.RunAsync() directly when the window is loaded.

Future enhancement may move start/stop control into the view model for better testability.

StepCommand

Gets the command to execute a single CPU instruction (step mode).

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> StepCommand { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that executes one instruction while paused.

Remarks

When executed, calls IEmulatorState.RequestStep() which runs exactly one CPU clock cycle. Useful for debugging and single-stepping through code.

StepOnce

Gets the command to execute a single CPU instruction.

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> StepOnce { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that steps one instruction while paused.

Remarks

Implementation Note: This command is currently a no-op placeholder. The actual step logic is handled by StepCommand which calls IEmulatorState.RequestStep().

This may be redundant with StepCommand and could be removed or consolidated.

StopEmu

Gets the command to stop emulator execution.

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> StopEmu { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that halts continuous emulation.

Remarks

Implementation Note: This command is currently a no-op placeholder. The actual stop logic is handled by the view which cancels the RunAsync() task.

Future enhancement may move start/stop control into the view model for better testability.

SystemStatus

Gets the view model for displaying system status (soft switches, buttons).

public SystemStatusViewModel SystemStatus { get; }

Property Value

SystemStatusViewModel

View model showing soft switch states and pushbutton status.

ThrottleEnabled

Gets or sets whether CPU throttling is enabled to maintain 1.023 MHz speed.

public bool ThrottleEnabled { get; set; }

Property Value

bool

True to run at Apple IIe speed, false to run as fast as possible.

Remarks

When true, the emulator runs at approximately 1.023 MHz (authentic Apple IIe speed). When false, runs unthrottled (useful for loading programs quickly or testing).

This property is bound to a menu item checkbox. Changes are observed by the view which propagates them to the VA2M.ThrottleEnabled property.

ToggleCapsLock

Gets the command to toggle Caps Lock emulation on/off.

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleCapsLock { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that inverts the CapsLockEnabled property.

Remarks

Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates CapsLockEnabled which the view observes to control keyboard input transformation.

ToggleDecreaseContrast

Gets the command to toggle reduced contrast display on/off.

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleDecreaseContrast { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that inverts the DecreaseContrast property.

Remarks

Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates DecreaseContrast which the view observes to control display intensity.

ToggleMonoMixed

Gets the command to toggle monochrome mixed mode on/off.

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleMonoMixed { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that inverts the MonoMixed property.

Remarks

Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates MonoMixed which the view observes to control mixed mode color rendering.

ToggleMonochrome

Gets the command to toggle monochrome display mode on/off.

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleMonochrome { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that inverts the ForceMonochrome property.

Remarks

Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates ForceMonochrome which the view observes to control color vs monochrome rendering.

ToggleScanLines

Gets the command to toggle scanline display effect on/off.

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleScanLines { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that inverts the ShowScanLines property.

Remarks

Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates ShowScanLines which the view observes to control display rendering.

ToggleSoftSwitchStatus

Gets the command to toggle soft switch status panel visibility on/off.

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleSoftSwitchStatus { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that inverts the ShowSoftSwitchStatus property.

Remarks

Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates ShowSoftSwitchStatus which the view observes to control status panel visibility.

ToggleThrottle

Gets the command to toggle CPU throttling on/off.

public ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit> ToggleThrottle { get; }

Property Value

ReactiveCommand<Unit, Unit>

Command that inverts the ThrottleEnabled property.

Remarks

Bound to menu item or keyboard shortcut. Updates ThrottleEnabled which the view observes to update VA2M.ThrottleEnabled.
